sections.heading: As the IFSC continues to evolve as a global ϐinancial hub, the sophistication of cyber
sections.summary: Cyber security is foundational for financial stability in IFSC. Financial entities must maintain robust cyber security to protect systems from threats impacting trust in the jurisdiction.
sections.dtia_summary: Cyber security is foundational for financial stability in IFSC. Financial entities must maintain robust cyber security to protect systems from threats impacting trust in the jurisdiction.

requirements.text: Cyber security is foundational for financial stability in IFSC

sections.source_quote: As the IFSC continues to evolve as a global financial hub, the sophistication of cyber threats targeting financial entities is also expected to grow.

sections.heading: The key components of the Guidelines are categorized into:
sections.summary: Guidelines are categorized into governance, cyber security framework, third party risk management, communication & awareness, and audit.
sections.source_quote: The key components of the Guidelines are categorized into: Governance, Cyber security and cyber resilience framework, Third party risk management, Communication & awareness, Audit

sections.heading: The REs shall have adequate governance mechanisms, with a clear set of roles and
sections.summary: REs must have clear governance mechanisms with an Oversight Body including governing board, senior management, or committees for cyber risk management.
sections.dtia_summary: REs must have clear governance mechanisms with an Oversight Body including governing board, senior management, or committees for cyber risk management.

requirements.text: REs shall have adequate governance mechanisms, with a clear set of roles and responsibilities to manage cyber risk

sections.source_quote: The REs shall have adequate governance mechanisms, with a clear set of roles and responsibilities to manage cyber risk.

sections.heading: The REs shall ensure that their Governing Board and the senior management possesses
sections.summary: REs must ensure governing board and senior management have expertise to manage cyber risk and cultivate a culture of cyber risk awareness.
sections.dtia_summary: REs must ensure governing board and senior management have expertise to manage cyber risk and cultivate a culture of cyber risk awareness.

requirements.text: REs shall ensure that their Governing Board and the senior management possesses sufficient expertise and knowledge to effectively understand and manage cyber risk

sections.source_quote: The REs shall ensure that their Governing Board and the senior management possesses sufficient expertise and knowledge to effectively understand and manage cyber risk.

sections.heading: The REs shall appoint a CISO or alternatively, designate a senior employee/
sections.summary: REs must appoint a CISO or designate a senior employee to assess, respond to incidents, and direct cyber security processes.
sections.dtia_summary: REs must appoint a CISO or designate a senior employee to assess, respond to incidents, and direct cyber security processes.

requirements.text: REs shall appoint a CISO or alternatively, designate a senior employee/ management personnel to assess, identify and reduce cyber security risks
REs shall appoint a CISO or alternatively, designate a senior employee/ management personnel to respond to incidents, establish appropriate standards and controls

REs shall appoint a CISO or alternatively, designate a senior employee/ management personnel to direct the establishment and implementation of processes and procedures as approved by the Oversight Body of the RE

sections.source_quote: The REs shall appoint a CISO or alternatively, designate a senior employee/ management personnel to assess, identify and reduce cyber security risks, respond to incidents, establish appropriate standards and controls, and direct the establishment and implementation of processes and procedures as approved by the Oversight Body of the RE

sections.heading: The CISO or senior employee/ management personnel so designated shall henceforth
sections.summary: The CISO or designated senior employee is referred to as the 'Designated Officer'.
sections.source_quote: The CISO or senior employee/ management personnel so designated shall henceforth be referred to as the 'Designated Officer'.

sections.heading: The REs shall formulate the Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework to
sections.summary: REs must formulate a Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework to maintain confidentiality, integrity, and availability of IT assets.
sections.dtia_summary: REs must formulate a Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework to maintain confidentiality, integrity, and availability of IT assets.

requirements.text: REs shall formulate the Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework to maintain the Confidentiality, Integrity and Availability of their IT assets

sections.source_quote: The REs shall formulate the Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience Framework to maintain the Confidentiality, Integrity and Availability of their IT assets.

sections.heading: The REs shall formulate an Information Security (IS) Policy as part of their cyber
sections.summary: REs must implement information security policies including asset identification, classification, protection, access control, physical security, vulnerability assessments, recovery, incident management, and audit trails.
sections.dtia_summary: REs must implement information security policies including asset identification, classification, protection, access control, physical security, vulnerability assessments, recovery, incident management, and audit trails.

requirements.text: REs shall maintain a detailed inventory of IT assets, including both logical (data, software) and physical (hardware) components
REs shall carry out a risk assessment of those assets and classify the assets based on their business criticality, sensitivity of data they hold, and potential impact on other systems if compromised
REs shall identify and classify their business functions and supporting processes based on their criticality to overall operations and potential impact on performance
REs shall implement appropriate security controls, aligned with international best practices and cyber security and cyber resilience standards like NIST, ISO 27000, etc
REs shall manage access rights to IT assets and their supporting systems on a 'need-to-know' basis, following the principle of 'least privilege' and principle of 'segregation of duties'
REs shall enforce authentication methods that are sufficiently robust to adequately and effectively ensure that access control policies and procedures are complied with
REs need to ensure adequate physical security of their IT assets, using measures such as secure location of critical data, restricted access to data centers, server rooms etc

REs shall conduct Vulnerability Assessment and Penetration Testing (VAPT) to detect vulnerabilities in the IT environment for all critical systems, infrastructure components and other IT systems at least once a year
REs shall have recovery policies and procedures to maximise their ability to provide services on an ongoing basis and to limit losses in the event of severe business disruption
REs shall develop and implement processes for preventing, detecting, analysing and responding to cyber incidents

REs shall ensure that audit trail exists for IT assets, such that it satisfies the entity's business continuity and recovery needs, regulatory and legal obligations, facilitates audit and serves as forensic evidence when required

sections.source_quote: The REs shall formulate an Information Security (IS) Policy as part of their cyber security and cyber resilience framework with the following basic principles.

sections.heading: The REs shall adopt a collaborative security approach with their third-party
sections.summary: REs must adopt a collaborative security approach with third-party vendors by outlining shared expectations for data security, incident reporting, and compliance.
sections.dtia_summary: REs must adopt a collaborative security approach with third-party vendors by outlining shared expectations for data security, incident reporting, and compliance.

requirements.text: REs shall adopt a collaborative security approach with their third-party vendors/external partners, by clearly outlining shared expectations for data security, incident reporting and adherence to relevant security standards

sections.source_quote: The REs shall adopt a collaborative security approach with their third-party vendors/external partners, by clearly outlining shared expectations for data security, incident reporting and adherence to relevant security standards.

sections.heading: The REs shall adopt a risk-based approach for periodic review of third-party
sections.summary: REs must conduct risk-based periodic reviews of third-party vendors for vulnerabilities or compliance gaps every six months for critical services.
sections.dtia_summary: REs must conduct risk-based periodic reviews of third-party vendors for vulnerabilities or compliance gaps every six months for critical services.

requirements.text: REs shall identify the third-party service providers which it is dependent on for its core operations or to whom it has granted access to its critical systems
For all such third-party service providers, the REs shall carry out an assessment for detecting vulnerabilities or compliance gaps through audit, review or feedback every six months

sections.source_quote: The REs shall adopt a risk-based approach for periodic review of third-party vendors/external partners. The REs shall identify the third-party service providers which it is dependent on for its core operations or to whom it has granted access to its critical systems. For all such third-party service providers, the REs shall carry out an assessment for detecting vulnerabilities or compliance gaps through audit, review or feedback every six months.

sections.heading: The REs should establish clear communication channels and escalation procedures
sections.summary: REs must establish clear communication channels and escalation procedures for addressing risks or non-compliance with partners.
sections.dtia_summary: REs must establish clear communication channels and escalation procedures for addressing risks or non-compliance with partners.

requirements.text: REs should establish clear communication channels and escalation procedures for addressing any identified risks or non-compliance with partners, promptly and effectively

sections.source_quote: The REs should establish clear communication channels and escalation procedures for addressing any identified risks or non-compliance with partners, promptly and effectively.

sections.heading: The ultimate responsibility to mitigate the cyber risks arising from the third parties
sections.summary: REs bear ultimate responsibility for mitigating cyber risks arising from third parties.
sections.dtia_summary: REs bear ultimate responsibility for mitigating cyber risks arising from third parties.

requirements.text: The ultimate responsibility to mitigate the cyber risks arising from the third parties shall be on the REs

sections.source_quote: The ultimate responsibility to mitigate the cyber risks arising from the third parties shall be on the REs.

sections.heading: The REs shall provide regular training to its employees on topics pertaining to cyber
sections.summary: REs must provide regular cyber security training to employees on phishing, social engineering, password hygiene, and incident reporting.
sections.dtia_summary: REs must provide regular cyber security training to employees on phishing, social engineering, password hygiene, and incident reporting.

requirements.text: REs shall provide regular training to its employees on topics pertaining to cyber security, including but not limited to, phishing awareness, social engineering, password hygiene and incident reporting procedures

sections.source_quote: REs shall provide regular training to its employees on topics pertaining to cyber security, including but not limited to, phishing awareness, social engineering, password hygiene and incident reporting procedures.

sections.heading: The REs shall establish clear and accessible channels for employees to report
sections.summary: REs must provide regular cyber security training to employees on phishing, social engineering, password hygiene, and incident reporting.

sections.heading: The aim of the audit is to provide an independent assurance of the effectiveness of
sections.summary: REs must provide regular cyber security training to employees on phishing, social engineering, password hygiene, and incident reporting.

sections.heading: The auditor engaged by an RE shall not have any conϐlict of interest with the RE. The
sections.summary: REs must provide regular cyber security training to employees on phishing, social engineering, password hygiene, and incident reporting.

sections.heading: In case the RE is registered with IFSCA as a Broker Dealer/Bullion Trading Member,
sections.summary: REs must provide regular cyber security training to employees on phishing, social engineering, password hygiene, and incident reporting.

sections.heading: In case of occurrence of any cyber incident, the REs are required to report the
sections.summary: REs must provide regular cyber security training to employees on phishing, social engineering, password hygiene, and incident reporting.

sections.heading: Additionally, the REs shall submit the interim report within 3 days followed by a
sections.summary: REs must provide regular cyber security training to employees on phishing, social engineering, password hygiene, and incident reporting.

sections.heading: The following categories of REs are exempted from the requirements mentioned in
sections.summary: REs operating as branches of regulated entities, providing services to group entities only, with less than 10 employees, or foreign universities in IFSCs are exempted from requirements.
sections.dtia_summary: REs operating as branches of regulated entities, providing services to group entities only, with less than 10 employees, or foreign universities in IFSCs are exempted from requirements.

requirements.text: REs operating in the form of a branch of a regulated Indian or foreign entity are exempted
REs providing services to their group entities only e.g. Global In-House Centres (GICs) are exempted
REs which have less than 10 employees are exempted
Foreign universities set up in IFSCs are exempted

sections.source_quote: The following categories of REs are exempted from the requirements mentioned in these Guidelines: REs operating in the form of a branch of a regulated Indian or foreign entity, REs providing services to their group entities only e.g. Global In-House Centres (GICs), REs which have less than 10 employees, Foreign universities set up in IFSCs.

sections.heading: The exemptions to the REs provided vide para 21, are subject to fulϐilment of the
sections.summary: Exemptions require REs to adopt parent entity's cyber security framework, have parent entity's CISO as Designated Officer, and parent entity must be regulated by a financial-sector regulator.
sections.dtia_summary: Exemptions require REs to adopt parent entity's cyber security framework, have parent entity's CISO as Designated Officer, and parent entity must be regulated by a financial-sector regulator.

requirements.text: REs shall adopt the Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience framework and IS Policy of its parent entity
The CISO of the parent entity shall act as the Designated Officer for the REs in IFSC

The parent entity of REs, in India or overseas, shall be regulated by a financial-sector regulator in its home jurisdiction and cyber security and cyber resilience framework of the parent entity shall include within its scope, the REs in IFSC

sections.source_quote: The exemptions to the REs provided vide para 21, are subject to fulfilment of the following conditions: REs shall adopt the Cyber Security and Cyber Resilience framework and IS Policy of its parent entity, The CISO of the parent entity shall act as the Designated Officer for the REs in IFSC, The parent entity of REs, in India or overseas, shall be regulated by a financial-sector regulator in its home jurisdiction and cyber security and cyber resilience framework of the parent entity shall include within its scope, the REs in IFSC.
